name: Roasted-Red-Onion Ravioli
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons fresh thyme, minced (1 tablespoon dried thyme)
1 tablespoon Balsamic or red wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the Egg Pasta: Form into two balls; set aside.
2. Prepare the Filling:
a. Preheat oven to 375°F.
b. Mince fresh thyme (or use dried thyme).
c. Toss oil, onions, and salt with minced thyme in a shallow roasting pan.
d. Roast onions cut-side down for about 10 minutes until brown.
e. Remove onions from the pan and set aside to cool.
f. Deglaze the pan with vinegar using a wooden spoon.
g. Peel cooled onions and chop.
h. Combine chopped onions, pan juices, and dried thyme (if used) in a small bowl; season with salt and pepper.
3. Prepare the Pasta Dough:
a. Roll both dough balls as thin as possible into 2 rectangular sheets.
b. Cover one sheet with a damp towel and set aside.
c. Lightly brush the remaining sheet with beaten egg.
4. Assemble Ravioli:
a. Place about 12 teaspoons of filling on top of one egg-coated sheet, spaced about 2 inches apart.
b. Cover the filling with the second egg-coated sheet and press firmly around the mounds.
c. Cut between the mounds using a fluted ravioli cutter.
5. Cook Ravioli:
a. Place ravioli on lightly floured baking sheets or waxed paper; refrigerate if not cooking immediately.
6. Prepare Sauce:
a. In a saucepan, combine cream and fresh thyme; bring to simmer over medium heat.
b. Reduce the mixture to 1/2 cup for about 5 minutes.
c. Stir in butter; strain the sauce and season with salt and pepper.
7. Serve:
a. Cook ravioli in boiling, salted water until tender (about 6 minutes).
b. Drain well and toss with cream sauce and grated Parmesan cheese.
Makes 4 side-dish servings
[Attribution: COOKS, March-April 1988]
